capability_foundations.capability_label: snapshot isolation for all mutable state
Snapshot isolation for all mutable state

operating_model.rule: Regression baselines and smoke harnesses before enabling by default.
Distribution and release only after convergence and archive checks.

overlap_policy.description: SIGMA and UPSILON may overlap after governance vocabulary is frozen, because policy and control-plane artifacts reinforce each other.
PHI planning may overlap with SIGMA and UPSILON, but PHI implementation waits for fresh snapshot mapping.
ZETA work may overlap only as documentation and rehearsal design until phases A, B, and C complete.

post_snapshot_required_items.reason: Exact moves depend on the fresh repository snapshot and cannot be safely guessed from planning artifacts.
Render integration depends on actual runtime boundaries in the fresh snapshot.
Loader insertion points must be mapped against the live codebase after convergence and freeze work.
The lock artifact must be generated from the current build graph, not an older audit snapshot.
Exact CI and pipeline steps depend on the current repo layout and tool inventory.
Preset cleanup is repository-state sensitive and must be derived from the live tree.
Authority handoff cannot be planned as code without fresh runtime topology evidence.
Live cutovers require exact service boundaries and call sites from the live repo.
Replication topology depends on actual state partitions, services, and products.
Rollback validation must be wired to the current release, replay, and trust surfaces.

pre_snapshot_safe_items.reason: The agent context contract is a planning artifact and should be stabilized before code work.
Cutover grammar is safer to design before the repo-specific wiring begins.
Governance vocabulary, task catalogs, and operator policy are architecture-level and do not require exact insertion points.
Policy modeling is a doctrine problem first and should guide later implementation.
Task types, validation levels, and refusal expectations can be designed before the fresh snapshot arrives.
The component model should be decided as architecture doctrine before repository-specific implementation work.
Kernel doctrine, service boundaries, and lawful state movement can be specified before code insertion points are re-mapped.
Export/import semantics and lifecycle doctrine are design artifacts first.
Rollback and downgrade policy should lead implementation rather than follow it.
Versioning and release policy can be frozen ahead of repository-specific control-plane wiring.

items.summary: Mirror AGENTS governance into stable machine-readable workflows before automation expands.
items.title: Agent governance

items.summary: Create mirrored operator and agent surfaces so governance is inspectable from both human and tool paths.
items.title: Agent mirrors

items.summary: Bind natural-language tasks to deterministic task types, validations, and refusal paths.
items.title: Natural-language task bridge

items.summary: Freeze the kernel doctrine for services, truth separation, and lawful state movement.
items.title: Runtime kernel model

items.summary: Define component boundaries, identity, lifecycle hooks, and state ownership expectations.
items.title: Component model

items.summary: Introduce capability-negotiated module loading only after exact insertion points are mapped.
items.title: Module loader

items.summary: Separate service responsibilities from the kernel without violating process-only mutation.
items.title: Runtime services

items.summary: Model export/import, state ownership, and replay-safe transfer boundaries before live cutovers.
items.title: State externalization

items.summary: Lock the build graph so later control-plane work has a deterministic substrate.
items.title: Build graph lock

items.summary: Consolidate execution presets and toolchains into a governed release surface.
items.title: Preset and toolchain consolidation

items.summary: Freeze release numbering, compatibility, and migration obligations before rollout automation.
items.title: Versioning policy

items.summary: Refine release indexing and publication semantics before live control-plane work.
items.title: Release index policy refinement

priority_bands.label: Must happen before any live-runtime ambitions

items.summary: Add governed startup, shutdown, handoff, and rollback choreography for runtime services.
items.title: Lifecycle manager

items.summary: Record rollout, downgrade, and rollback intent in a deterministic control-plane ledger.
items.title: Release transaction log

items.summary: Govern staged exposure, quarantine, and reversal before live cutovers.
items.title: Canary and blue-green rollout policy

items.summary: Define rollback, yank, and degraded boot discipline before higher-risk replacement work.
items.title: Disaster downgrade policy

items.summary: Separate render intent from backend execution so renderer replacement becomes testable.
items.title: Framegraph

items.summary: Introduce a governed device abstraction before backend swap or mirrored execution work.
items.title: Render device abstraction

items.summary: Define replacement boundaries and state handoff contracts before any live replacement feature work.
items.title: Hotswap boundaries

items.summary: Introduce controlled isolation before untrusted mod or sidecar work.
items.title: Sandboxing

items.summary: Support controlled version overlap before live protocol, ABI, or content cutovers.
items.title: Multi-version coexistence

items.summary: Stabilize asset and shader ingestion before live mount, streaming, or mirrored execution.
items.title: Asset pipeline

items.summary: Enable service restarts, controlled replacement, and shadow cutovers only after A, B, and C complete.
items.title: Early replaceability features

items.summary: Allow pack mount and unmount only after compatibility, rollback, and isolation controls exist.
items.title: Content live mount and unmount

items.summary: Add side-by-side validation services after service boundaries and proof hooks are frozen.
items.title: Mirrored execution sidecars

items.summary: Expand to deterministic distributed runtime only after replay, handoff, and quorum proofs exist.
items.title: Distributed simulation

items.summary: Move state across shards only after deterministic replication and authority handoff are proven.
items.title: Live shard relocation

items.summary: Evolve protocol and schema live only after version coexistence and rollback proofs are stable.
items.title: Protocol and schema live evolution

items.summary: Treat core replacement as a last-stage research problem, not an early delivery target.
items.title: Restartless core engine replacement

items.summary: Consider cluster-of-clusters only after deterministic distributed simulation is already boring and proven.
items.title: Cluster-of-clusters

series_execution_order.why_now: Governance, operator policy, and validation mapping must exist before aggressive automation or live cutovers.
Release control, rollback policy, and deterministic distribution govern every later replacement workflow.
Componentization is required for replaceability, but exact insertion points must wait for the fresh snapshot mapping.
Live runtime operations are downstream of governance, control-plane, and runtime component foundations.
